Name: latency_aware_pac_bayes_inference
Title: Harnessing PAC-Bayes Frameworks for Latency-Aware Machine Learning Inference
Short Hypothesis: Integrating PAC-Bayes principles with a latency-aware, training-free framework will enhance real-time inference performance while maintaining strict adherence to latency budgets.
Related Work: Existing literature has explored cloud-based inference optimization, federated learning with uncertainty, and calibration techniques. However, there is a significant gap in combining these elements with PAC-Bayes frameworks to ensure both empirical validation and strict latency adherence during inference. Most works fail to address low-latency constraints without incurring training overhead or compromising model performance.
Abstract: This proposal outlines a novel framework that synergistically combines PAC-Bayes principles with a latency-aware design for machine learning inference. The research aims to establish a training-free methodology that ensures real-time performance while maintaining strict adherence to latency budgets. By leveraging a Jacobian-based layer-skipping mechanism, we will derive enforceable protocols to guarantee bounded overhead and minimal perturbation during inference. The framework will be empirically validated against existing benchmarks, focusing on throughput-accuracy trade-offs, quantify perturbation costs, and memory bandwidth. This research not only fills a critical gap in existing literature but also contributes to the development of rigorous, compute-frugal inference protocols suitable for latency-sensitive applications.
Experiments: 1. Implement a PAC-Bayes-based layer-skipping mechanism on standard classification datasets (e.g., CIFAR-10, MNIST) and measure inference latency under various load conditions.
2. Evaluate the throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier by contrasting the proposed method with existing latency-aware models.
3. Validate the overhead incurred by implementing the PAC-Bayes constraints through empirical measurements of wall-clock time and memory usage.
4. Conduct a sensitivity analysis on the impact of various latency thresholds to identify optimal configurations for different real-world applications.
Risk Factors and Limitations: 1. Potential trade-offs between latency and accuracy may arise, necessitating careful calibration of the framework.
2. The generalizability of the proposed framework across diverse datasets and real-world scenarios may need further validation.
3. Reliance on empirical benchmarking may introduce variability based on hardware specifications and implementation details.
